skill.name: code-refactoring-tech-debt
skill.description: Analyze technical debt, quantify its impact, and produce prioritized refactoring plans with measurable remediation steps.
skill.summary: Creates practical technical debt inventories, impact estimates, and refactoring roadmaps.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick and weak cryptography patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences and ordinary prose. The skill is an instruction-only technical debt analysis guide with no executable scripts, network calls, or data exfiltration behavior. Publishable with a low risk note because it asks agents to inspect codebases and may encourage use of local quality tooling.

low_findings.title: Static command-execution matches are documentation examples
low_findings.description: The detected Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown code fences showing sample debt reports, dashboards, and remediation plans. They are not executable skill code and do not contain command execution instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are fenced Markdown examples and structured prose, not runnable Ruby or shell code. No instruction tells the agent to execute these snippets.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography matches are word-boundary false positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography matches occur in normal technical debt prose, such as design guidance and documentation references. No cryptographic algorithm implementation or recommendation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text, not cryptographic code or instructions. The pattern appears to match substrings inside ordinary words.

low_findings.title: Broad repository inspection guidance
low_findings.description: The skill directs agents to analyze codebases for technical debt and quantify findings. This is expected for the skill, but users should run it only on repositories they are authorized to inspect.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicit in the skill instructions and is legitimate for code quality work. The risk depends on user authorization and workspace boundaries rather than malicious content.

content.value_statement: Technical debt slows delivery and increases defects when teams cannot measure its impact. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code identify debt, estimate cost, and plan practical remediation.
actual_capabilities: Identifies code, architecture, testing, documentation, and infrastructure debt categories.
Quantifies impact using time, cost, defect, and velocity estimates.
Builds KPI dashboards for complexity, duplication, coverage, and dependency health.
Prioritizes remediation by risk, effort, savings, and return on investment.
Creates prevention plans with quality gates, standards, and review practices.
limitations: Requires access to the relevant codebase, metrics, or user-provided context.
Estimates depend on assumptions about rates, defect costs, and team workflow.
Does not execute analysis tools by itself unless the host agent runs them.
Cannot replace human architecture review for high-risk system changes.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Refactoring Roadmap
use_cases.description: Turn scattered maintenance concerns into a prioritized plan with cost estimates and delivery milestones.

use_cases.title: Audit a Legacy Module
use_cases.description: Map complexity, duplication, coupling, and missing tests before starting a risky rewrite or migration.

use_cases.title: Explain Debt to Stakeholders
use_cases.description: Convert engineering concerns into business impact, risk levels, and measurable investment options.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Debt Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this repository for technical debt. Summarize the top issues, explain their impact, and suggest the first three improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritized Remediation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the codebase for technical debt. Estimate effort, risk, monthly cost, and ROI for each major issue. Return a prioritized roadmap.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture and Testing Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate architecture debt, dependency health, test coverage gaps, and complex code hotspots. Separate quick wins from quarterly initiatives.

prompt_templates.title: Executive Debt Briefing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a stakeholder-ready technical debt report with inventory, impact model, investment options, prevention gates, and measurable success metrics.

output_examples.input: A team asks why feature work is slowing in a legacy payment module.
output: Debt inventory grouped by code, architecture, testing, and documentation issues.
Impact estimate showing maintenance hours, defect costs, and risk severity.
Roadmap with quick wins, medium-term refactors, and prevention gates.

output_examples.input: A technical lead needs a plan before upgrading an old frontend stack.
output: Version lag and deprecated API assessment.
Migration plan with effort ranges, compatibility risks, and test requirements.
Success metrics for performance, coverage, and developer experience.

output_examples.input: An engineering manager needs a business case for refactoring time.
output: Executive summary of current debt and delivery impact.
ROI model comparing remediation cost against expected savings.
Quarterly plan with milestones and measurable outcomes.

best_practices: Provide real repository context, recent incidents, and any available engineering metrics.
Ask for evidence, estimates, and assumptions to be separated clearly.
Review proposed refactors with maintainers before changing critical systems.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to justify broad rewrites without measurable evidence.
Do not treat estimated savings as exact financial results.
Do not run analysis on code you are not authorized to inspect.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes code, architecture, testing, documentation, infrastructure, and process debt.

faq.question: Can it calculate exact debt cost?
faq.answer: It can estimate cost using provided assumptions, but exact cost requires real team and incident data.

faq.question: Does it change code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It produces analysis and plans unless the user separately asks the host agent to edit files.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is written for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Is it useful for small projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It can focus on quick wins, missing tests, duplication, and maintainability risks.

faq.question: How should teams use the output?
faq.answer: Teams should validate findings, choose high-value improvements, and track progress with agreed metrics.
